description: Lock the broad nanotechnology node to established nanoscale science and engineering rather than a 2035 forecast or molecular-assembler roadmap claim.

checks.reason: The node is scoped to the established, coordinated nanoscale science and engineering field around the National Nanotechnology Initiative era.
The 1974 early-research node is the historical predecessor for the established field.
High-resolution microscopy enabled nanoscale observation and measurement but is not the only path into the field.
Materials science is a central neighboring discipline, not a hard component prerequisite.
Chemistry is a shared nanoscale science foundation, not the sole prerequisite for the field.
Quantum effects help explain nanoscale phenomena but should not be modeled as a product-style required edge.
The generic microscope edge was replaced by the more specific electron-microscope enabling edge.
Electronics is too broad and not a direct prerequisite for the established nanoscale field.
Molecular biology is a major application/intersection area, not a direct prerequisite for the whole field.
Photolithography supports some nanoelectronics paths but is not a prerequisite for nanotechnology as a broad field.
